mnemonics: SPAN Sandwich: To SPAN a space, you need a good combination of ingredients (vectors) - like a sandwich needs bread, meat, and cheese.
keyPoints: A linear combination is an expression constructed from a set of terms by multiplying each term by a constant and adding the results.
The set of all possible linear combinations of a set of vectors is called their span.
A vector is said to be linearly dependent if it can be written as a linear combination of the other vectors in the set; otherwise, it is independent.
commonMistakes: Confusing linear independence with orthogonality (orthogonal vectors are independent, but independent vectors are not necessarily orthogonal).
Assuming that if a vector is a linear combination of others, it must be a scalar multiple of just one of them (it can be a combination of several).
